title: StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs, and Controller Selection · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective controller selection and completion contract; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in statefulsets, daemonsets, jobs, cronjobs, and controller selection, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in controller selection and completion contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Design StatefulSet ordinal identity, headless discovery, stable claims, ordering, update partition, scaling, and failure recovery around application guarantees.
Use DaemonSets for node-local facilities with selectors, tolerations, priority, rolling updates, resource bounds, and control-plane coverage decisions.
Model Job completions, parallelism, indexed work, retries, backoff, deadlines, success policy, duplicate execution, and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-controller-choice-defense-1x5ei". Tailor its controller-selection.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Controller Choice Defense into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense, keep K8s Controller Choice Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-controller-choice-defense-1x5ei
  - competency: k8s-controller-choice-defense
    artifact: controller-selection.yaml
    decision: controller choice defense
    environment: kubeadm recovery cluster restored onto replacement nodes
    fault-injection: fill ephemeral storage before graceful termination
    retained-proof: bound claim snapshot checksum and application consistency query
    case-key: 1x5ei
    change: |
      stateless-api: Deployment
      identity-bound-worker: StatefulSet
      node-agent: DaemonSet
      one-time-migration: Job
      scheduled-reconcile: CronJob
      selection-proof: identity-completion-placement-update
    verify: kubectl get deploy,sts,ds,job,cronjob -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject Deployment-for-everything, exactly-once Job claims, CronJob clock certainty, or StatefulSet as automatic data replication.
    repair: Select from identity, completion, placement, update, storage, retry, idempotency, and failure evidence; then test controller-specific recovery.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense, keep K8s Controller Choice Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, bare Pod, or custom controller selection from identity, completion, placement, update, and ownership evidence.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Controller Choice Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug platform runs a durable database as a Deployment, a node log agent as one replica, and a finite migration as a long-running Pod. Choose Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, or CronJob from identity, placement, completion, concurrency, and retry contracts. Competency probe: Defend Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, CronJob, bare Pod, or custom controller selection from identity, completion, placement, update, and ownership evidence.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Controller Choice Defense, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Deployment is the safest default for every long-running or finite workload.
